title: Elysée Palace searched as part of investigation into events contracts
subhead: The judiciary is examining how several public contracts were awarded to Shortcut Events, the company responsible in recent years for organizing major events for the president. Two investigating judges went to the palace on Thursday.

title: Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
Canal+ 'blacklist': Over half of recent French films credit people now threatened with retribution
Why Airbus, Air France were convicted on appeal in 2009 Rio-Paris crash
subhead: The two aviation giants were convicted of manslaughter in the accident of flight AF447, which killed 228 people on June 1, 2009. Both companies had previously been found not guilty at the first trial. They are now filing an appeal with France's highest court.

title: Major immigration study shows 'great blending' is underway in France, but inequalities persist
subhead: The second edition of a large-scale sociological survey reveals a rapid convergence in the cultural and social practices of immigrants and their descendants with those of the wider population. However, ongoing difficulties in access to employment and housing confirm the experience of systemic discrimination and racism.

title: War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two different worlds just 10 kilometers apart
subhead: On the front line, the small town of Oleshky in the south has been occupied by the Russian military since the invasion began. Residents try to reach Kherson at great personal risk.

title: Israel has damaged or destroyed 45% of towns in southern Lebanon
subhead: According to an exclusive analysis of radar satellite images conducted by Le Monde and Masae Analytics, nearly half of the urban centers in southern Lebanon have been affected or even destroyed since the beginning of Israel's ground offensive in March 2026.

In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
subhead: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

title: JR covers Pont-Neuf bridge in Paris with giant inflatable cave
subhead: The temporary installation 'La Caverne du Pont-Neuf' has been set up on the Paris landmark since Thursday. The site will be open to the public free of charge, 24 hours a day, for three weeks from June 6.

title: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.
